g.text: Memory Transaction Granularity: GPU L2 Cache Interface

div.p: Workgroup: 32 Threads (Warp) | Payload: 32 x float32 (4 bytes) = 128 Bytes
Efficiency: 100% Adjacent threads $T_n$ access adjacent addresses $A_n$. Memory Controller combines all 32 requests into a single 128B burst.
Efficiency: 3.125% Threads access memory with Stride $\ge$ 128B. Hardware is forced to fetch a full 128B line for every single 4B request.

ol.li: Granularity: The Memory Controller physical interface is fixed at 128-byte segments.
Alignment: Transactions cannot cross 128B boundaries; requests crossing boundaries are split.
Coalescing Unit: Logic sits between the Warp Scheduler and L2 Cache to detect address continuity.
Padding Cost: Unused bytes in a fetched cache line still consume bus cycle time and power.
